channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Vancouver Launches Six-Month Trial for Food Delivery Robots in Downtown and Kitsilano
item.description: Vancouver city councillors have approved a six-month pilot program to test food delivery robots in the downtown and Kitsilano neighbourhoods starting this fall. The initiative, run by U.S.-based Serve...

item.title: Wheelchair User Finally Moves into Accessible Home After Six-Year Wait in Motherwell
item.description: Thobile Saza, a wheelchair user, and his family have finally moved into a proper five-room home in Motherwell, Nelson Mandela Bay, more than six years after being relocated to temporary housing. Durin...

item.title: Kenyan Man Returns Financed Car to Bank After Struggling With Loan Repayments
item.description: A young Kenyan man has sparked serious conversation online after choosing to voluntarily return his car to the bank due to the pressure of loan repayment. Instead of waiting for repossession agents to...

item.title: Philippines Pushes for ASEAN Maritime Cooperation and Establishment of Maritime Center
item.description: During the 48th ASEAN Summit held in Cebu, President Ferdinand “Bongbong” Marcos Jr. expressed optimism about the adoption of the ASEAN Leaders' Declaration on Maritime Cooperation. The declaration em...

item.title: Top Tennis Players Seek Greater Share of Grand Slam Prize Money
item.description: Jannik Sinner has publicly joined other top tennis players in raising concerns over the distribution of prize money at major tournaments, particularly the French Open. While organisers announced a 10 ...

item.title: China sentences ex-defence ministers to death with reprieve over graft
item.description: The Chinese government under President Xi Jinping don sentence two former defence ministers, Wei Fenghe and Li Shangfu, to death, but with two-year reprieve, for serious corruption. Dis one kain sente...

item.title: Freedom Day Reflections: Assessing Social and Economic Progress 32 Years into Democracy
item.description: On Freedom Day 2026, the Inkatha Freedom Party (IFP) addressed the progress and challenges of South Africa's democracy, 32 years after the country’s first non-racial elections in 1994. Honourable Mini...

item.title: FBI Director Kash Patel Faces Scrutiny Over Polygraph Orders for Security Staff
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel is facing criticism after reports emerged that he ordered polygraph tests for over a dozen members of his security detail, both current and former. According to MS NOW, Patel i...

item.title: IEA urges Canada to accelerate energy development and exports amid favourable global market
item.description: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has urged Canada to accelerate the development and export of its energy resources, warning that current favourable market conditions may not last. IEA Executive D...

item.title: Postmortem Reveals PCEA Pastor Julius Ndumia Died From Asphyxiation and Head Injuries
item.description: Nakuru residents are still shaken after the tragic death of PCEA Tabuga pastor Julius Ndumia, who was found dead on May 4, 2026, within the church compound in Lanet. A postmortem examination by pathol...

item.title: Motorcycle Rider Killed During Superbike Qualifying at North West 200
item.description: A tragic incident occurred during Superbike qualifying at the North West 200 international road race in Northern Ireland on Thursday, resulting in the death of a rider. The crash took place at Station...

item.title: Rethinking the Philippines’ Energy Strategy Amid Global Fuel Volatility
item.description: The Philippines is facing a critical energy challenge due to decades of underinvestment in domestic, fixed-cost energy sources, leaving households vulnerable to fluctuations in global fuel prices. The...

item.title: Pakistan coordinates with Singapore to ensure safe return of nationals from US-seized vessels
item.description: On Friday, Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar confirmed that he spoke with Singaporean Foreign Minister Vivian Balakrishnan to request assistance for the welfare and repatriation of ...

item.title: NAB Attaches Multiple Bahria Town Properties Over Money Laundering Allegations
item.description: The National Accountability Bureau (NAB) in Karachi has taken action to freeze four high-value Bahria Town properties, following directives from an accountability court. This step comes under the Anti...

item.title: Benazir Nashonuma Programme Shows Significant Improvement in Child and Maternal Health
item.description: According to a recent study conducted by Aga Khan University, Pakistan has seen a notable reduction in child stunting under the Benazir Nashonuma Programme (BNP). The evaluation revealed that children...

item.title: Kylie Kelce Reacts Lightly After 'Jeopardy!' Contestants Fail to Recognize Her
item.description: Kylie Kelce, podcast host and wife of former NFL player Jason Kelce, recently responded with humor and indifference after being the subject of a missed clue on the quiz show “Jeopardy!”. During an Apr...

item.title: Two women linked to Islamic State face slavery charges in Melbourne
item.description: Two women with known ties to the Islamic State have been formally charged with offences related to slavery after arriving in Melbourne on Thursday night. The charges are part of ongoing investigations...

item.title: Thati Zulu Prepares for Wings for Life World Run, Bringing Hockey Skills to Endurance Running
item.description: South African hockey standout Onthatile 'Thati' Zulu is trading in her hockey stick for running shoes as she gears up for the Wings for Life World Run this weekend. As a Red Bull Athlete, Thati is emb...

item.title: Aisha Yesufu Plans To Channel Her Activism Into Political Office In FCT
item.description: Aisha Yesufu, the activist well known for co-leading the #BringBackOurGirls campaign, don talk say her step into politics no mean say she dey drop activism or advocacy. For interview on Channels Telev...

item.title: White House criticizes Mark Hamill's social media post about Trump
item.description: A recent social media post by actor Mark Hamill, known for his role in Star Wars, sparked a sharp response from the White House. Hamill shared content related to the grave of former President Donald T...
